steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: one ending with “Pueblo Revolt of ______,” another starting with “the Spanish colonizers were expelled.” A dependent prepositional phrase sits between them.

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses require a period, semicolon, or comma plus coordinating conjunction to be properly joined.

Choice D supplies 1680, and, providing the comma and coordinating conjunction to join these clauses correctly.

The other three choices either create a comma splice (A, C) or are nonsensical (B).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
